125770.explanation: It would be nice if this could succeed. For us, the word ‘should’ should be replaced by the word ‘aspire’.

125771.explanation: We strongly support involving residents as much as possible on all topics.

125774.explanation: Provided good agreements are made about size and spatial integration.

125775.explanation: Support can also be provided by making personnel and equipment available.

125778.explanation: This mainly depends on the size of the company and the possibilities employers have or receive from the municipality.

125782.explanation: Is not a municipal task, especially with all our small villages. It is an idea to reduce nuisance though.

125783.explanation: This can simply be brought to the yard.

125786.explanation: Within the legal frameworks of the environmental vision and plan this is limitedly possible. Farmers should not become developers of real estate.

125787.explanation: However, this already happens through the Leergeld Foundation.

125788.explanation: But then more local news from Vijfheerenlanden must be broadcast.

125790.explanation: This already happens with Music at School, which the municipality partly subsidizes.

125795.explanation: With due observance of the privacy law.

125802.explanation: Not everyone is able to do this, it depends on their condition. Encouraging meaningful social work is very important!!

125804.explanation: We have already done this. We now have 130%.

125806.explanation: This should only happen when it is necessary.

125808.explanation: Only when this is practically possible.

125813.explanation: In some villages, a business park can be added or replaced. We are not necessarily in favor of more business parks.

125814.explanation: The shopping Sundays are already well arranged and this works well.

125817.explanation: It looks bad and causes clutter in the landscape.

125821.explanation: The policy states that only one is allowed.

125823.explanation: Does not fit in the landscape of a rural municipality. This could be possible in Leerdam and Vianen though.

125825.explanation: Every village must have its own center.

125828.explanation: The Voorstraat can be adjusted regarding parking by creating clear parking spaces and clearly indicating what areas are intended for pedestrians and other traffic. It makes no sense to make the entire center car-free because then the residents of the center are strongly disadvantaged.

125830.explanation: But only if that is possible and cooperation exists from property owners. This does not always succeed. The advantage of one large building is that all costs fall entirely on the COA and not on the municipality.

125770.explanation: We consider quality, experience, and a good reflection of society important. A balanced male-female ratio is desirable, but a strict 50/50 requirement can come at the expense of the best composition. Choose the strongest team, with diversity as a goal.

125771.explanation: We want to involve residents early through neighborhood and village councils, citizens' initiatives, and the Right to Challenge. The council is elected to weigh options and take responsibility. In very exceptional cases, a referendum can be chosen.

125774.explanation: We give farmers room for secondary activities and extra income, such as farm shops or small-scale recreation. But 'always' is not possible: it must fit within the environmental plan, traffic, safety, and nature, and have support from the surrounding area to prevent nuisance.

125775.explanation: Our election program states that we stimulate 'energy cooperatives at a truly local level' and that the municipality facilitates sustainable initiatives (including free energy coaches); financial support can be part of this, but not as an automatic obligation in all cases.

125776.explanation: Our program states that 'scarce space' requires 'clear choices' and that we protect nature and landscape through 'good spatial planning'; moreover, we focus on 'high-quality companies' and 'quality' in our business landscapes—so not automatically blocking everything, but steering strictly.

125778.explanation: Our election program states that 'the employer is responsible for housing their migrant workers' and that we 'do not accommodate more labor migrants than work in the municipality itself'; but requiring this always to be on the company premises goes too far for us.

125782.explanation: CDA wants a safer New Year's Eve. Firework shows are allowed, but preferably organized through permitted local organizers (association/neighborhood). The municipality does not need to organize itself, but should facilitate (location, permit, communication) if there is budget and support.

125783.explanation: CDA wants to separate waste and keep costs fair. Completely free bulky waste collection at home increases waste charges. Better: we maintain free bulky waste drop-off at the recycling yard.

125786.explanation: Farmers need prospects for the future, but building in rural areas is only allowed if it fits with the landscape and rules. So not just anywhere; there is room for custom solutions (e.g., farm house/farmyard) with clear conditions and integration.

125787.explanation: Participation is important, but the municipality does not always have to pay the full membership fees. Better: support via minimum income schemes/Youth Fund/Sports Fund for (part of) the contributions, with clear conditions and easy application.

125788.explanation: Municipal funds are scarce and go first to core tasks (care, safety, maintenance, facilities). Extra subsidies can threaten independence. However, we do facilitate with information and cooperation.

125790.explanation: We want to encourage music education at school with associations and keep arts/culture accessible. But a general municipal subsidy for every child is too broad and expensive. Focus support on children who cannot afford it (child package/Learning money/Avres).

125795.explanation: Camera surveillance can help with detection and reducing crime. We explicitly mention this as a tool in our program. Always targeted and proportionate, with privacy safeguards.

125802.explanation: Social assistance is a safety net, but we expect people who can to participate. Therefore, we focus on work arrangements, bonuses, and work experience places so residents distant from the labor market can work and gain experience. Always with customization.

125804.explanation: Many households have financial problems while schemes just miss them. We want basic security and access to essential facilities with simple schemes and one desk. For this reason, Vijfheerenlanden now has an income threshold of 130% of the social assistance standard. CDA supports this.

125806.explanation: We focus on sustainable mobility, including electric transport. That requires sufficient charging infrastructure in public spaces, especially in neighborhoods where people have no private driveway. Placement must be smart and have support so parking remains available for everyone.

125808.explanation: Care must fit the person and respect each individual’s culture or belief, but the municipality cannot guarantee 'always' choice. We want clear Wmo/youth care with fewer providers and appropriate, accessible care close to home.

125813.explanation: CDA Vijfheerenlanden primarily chooses to better utilize and improve quality of existing business parks. Expansion is only possible if demonstrably necessary for local employment or company growth, and fits within a careful consideration of space and livability.

125814.explanation: CDA Vijfheerenlanden is cautious about expanding shopping Sundays. Retailers should have room to conduct business, but Sunday rest, volunteer work, and family life are important. Shopping Sundays should not become an obligation and require local support.

125817.explanation: CDA Vijfheerenlanden wants agricultural land primarily used for food production. Solar panels on fertile farmland harm the open landscape and put farmers under pressure. Sustainable energy should mainly be on roofs, business parks, and alongside infrastructure, not on good farmland.

125821.explanation: CDA Vijfheerenlanden does not want to allow new coffeeshops. Our program chooses safety and livability in neighborhoods and villages. A coffeeshop increases the chance of nuisance and undermining and does not fit a safe, pleasant living environment for residents.

125823.explanation: CDA chooses building that suits both the village and historic character of our smaller cores and the more urbanized centers. Housing in Vijfheerenlanden requires customization: mainly low-rise and mixed housing forms and in specific cases high-rise that fits the surroundings.

125825.explanation: This is already present in the larger villages. It is important that facilities are not only in the cities but also in the villages. The larger villages have a higher number of facilities and will have a 'center' with various shops. The municipality facilitates this.

125828.explanation: CDA Vijfheerenlanden believes the center of Vianen must remain accessible for residents, entrepreneurs, and visitors. A completely car-free center limits accessibility and can harm local shops. Traffic changes may only be made with support and attention to accessibility.

125830.explanation: CDA Vijfheerenlanden takes responsibility for asylum accommodation and supports the Distribution Act. By spreading accommodation over the municipality where possible, we prevent overburdening one core. A condition is that accommodation is decent and fits the capacity of facilities.

125770.explanation: The leading principle is quality and suitability to perform the office, alongside a good reflection of society.

125771.explanation: Every four years, residents elect a council that sets policy frameworks on their behalf and oversees the implementation.

125774.explanation: Under conditions of landscape integration and economic feasibility, ample opportunities must be available and applications handled quickly.

125775.explanation: We do not see this as a municipal task.

125776.explanation: Available business parks must generate sufficient jobs to stimulate local employment as much as possible.

125778.explanation: As a first assignment, yes; but if this is not feasible within regulations, the entrepreneur must arrange housing elsewhere at their own expense.

125782.explanation: We believe in the organizational strength of villages and neighborhoods. The municipality must optimally facilitate in conditions and rules.

125783.explanation: There are fine options for residents to deliver bulky waste themselves to the recycling center. Collection against cost price must remain possible.

125786.explanation: This is only possible based on spatial feasibility and provincial environmental policy.

125787.explanation: Sports are important for children’s development. If associations cannot accommodate people receiving social assistance, support from the municipality can be requested.

125788.explanation: By offering added value, a local broadcaster should be able to function without extra government funds.

125790.explanation: We do not see music lessons as a municipal task, but they can fit well into school education programs.

125795.explanation: Everyone deserves a safe living environment and camera surveillance adds value here.

125802.explanation: We believe in work as a meaningful use of time and a springboard to paid employment.

125804.explanation: We believe in stimulating people towards paid employment.

125806.explanation: The starting point must be a realistic coverage relative to the number of electric cars in a neighborhood.

125808.explanation: Healthcare providers in the Netherlands are characterized by high quality and empathy in order to deliver the best care in a manner tailored to the care recipient.

125813.explanation: Due to its strategic location between the A2, A27, and A15 highways, Vijfheerenlanden is a suitable place to establish businesses, and space for growth for existing entrepreneurs is important.

125814.explanation: We want to fully deregulate Sunday opening hours and leave the decision to individual entrepreneurs.

125817.explanation: In our landscape policy, solar installations on the ground are only allowed near homes if placement on buildings is impossible.

125821.explanation: Current facilities are sufficient. Only if it demonstrably contributes to safety and livability without causing nuisance to residents can it be considered.

125823.explanation: High residential towers do not fit within the established visual quality requirements in the villages and large parts of the urban areas.

125825.explanation: Policy for the larger (facility) villages focuses on maintaining and where possible expanding facilities. This is not uniquely linked to a village center.

125828.explanation: Accessibility and safety must be leading principles.

125830.explanation: Small-scale (up to a maximum of one hundred residents) and spread throughout the municipality to improve support and integration.

125770.explanation: The Christian Union strives for an equitable distribution of men and women in the new council, from the perspective of equality and representativeness.

125771.explanation: The Christian Union wants to warmly welcome resident initiatives and stimulate citizen participation. However, it ensures that the municipal council (as the democratically elected body) is very clear from the start about the interplay and the space available for participation.

125774.explanation: The Christian Union supports 'short chains' (food directly from farmer to consumer) and (small-scale) recreational opportunities at farms.

125775.explanation: Energy cooperatives bring the energy transition closer to residents and enable local ownership. Therefore, the Christian Union wants the municipality to not only financially support such cooperatives but also assist with information provision. This also applies to smaller initiatives at home.

125776.explanation: The Christian Union wants the municipality of Vijfheerenlanden to stimulate establishment policies for companies whose employment matches local needs.

125778.explanation: For agricultural entrepreneurs, the rule applies that they must in principle provide housing on their own premises, so that regular housing remains available for others. The Christian Union voted in favor of these rules.

125782.explanation: The Christian Union favors a (national) fireworks ban. A fireworks show can be an attractive alternative, but is far too costly in every community.

125783.explanation: Waste processor Waardlanden collects bulky waste by appointment (free of charge). The Christian Union wants this service to remain in place.

125786.explanation: If agricultural buildings become available, the Christian Union believes it should be possible for farmers to make the location suitable for living, working, or recreation. What is possible differs per location and is laid down in clear rules.

125787.explanation: Also in the coming period, the Christian Union wants to structurally invest in the Poverty and Debt action plan as established in the municipality of Vijfheerenlanden, so that the goals and actions can be implemented.

125788.explanation: Local media are under pressure, while they are essential for democracy. Therefore, the Christian Union wants to support local media (financially) where possible.

125790.explanation: Music education is supported through the subsidy for Arts and Culture. The Christian Union wants to maintain this subsidy.

125795.explanation: Camera surveillance is a fine tool to use in unsafe situations. However, the Christian Union wants to apply it in a limited (proportional) manner and embed it within proper legislation (privacy).

125802.explanation: In Vijfheerenlanden, the Christian Union wants maximal space for alternative reintegration trajectories and forms of deregulated social assistance. For example, by establishing low-regulation zones, allowing part-time work, and having clear policies regarding gifts.

125804.explanation: For assistance with poverty in Vijfheerenlanden, the income threshold is already relatively high: 130% of the social assistance standard. As long as the necessity of a further increase is not demonstrated, the Christian Union wants to maintain this standard.

125806.explanation: If charging on private property is not possible, then in Vijfheerenlanden a public charging point is available within a radius of 250-500 meters. The Christian Union wants to expand the number of charging stations and follow the growth in the number of electric cars.

125808.explanation: The Christian Union wants the use of personal budget care (pgb) to remain possible and wants residents to be aware of this option. This way, care recipients always have the possibility to purchase identity-bound or other necessary care if it is not contracted.

125813.explanation: Locations near Vianen (Gaasperwaard) and Meerkerk should, according to the Christian Union, be developed, also to give entrepreneurs from Vijfheerenlanden more opportunities.

125814.explanation: The Christian Union is not in favor of further easing of Sunday opening hours for shops.

125817.explanation: The energy transition demands our full commitment. However, solar meadows are not part of the Christian Union's approach, although possibly under wind turbines to optimize the electricity connection.

125821.explanation: According to the Christian Union, no new coffeeshops will be added in Vijfheerenlanden.

125823.explanation: The Christian Union is not in favor of high residential towers, but rather housing suitable for the rural area.

125825.explanation: A community center in every neighborhood and village is essential for the Christian Union to provide space for social interaction. Additionally, a basic level of facilities (such as shops) is important for the livability of villages.

125828.explanation: By making the center of Vianen less car-friendly (linked to creating sufficient parking on the edge of the inner city), we make Vianen more attractive to stay in.

125830.explanation: Although the Christian Union prefers dispersed and small-scale accommodation, a larger location is also negotiable. Provided this does not disproportionately burden the capacity of existing networks and facilities in neighborhoods and villages.

125770.explanation: D66 wants a good balance between men and women in the council. Different people bring better ideas. But it also depends on who is available and what someone can do. Therefore, it should not be an obligation but remain an aim.

125771.explanation: D66 considers resident input important. For major plans, a referendum can help. It is important to always choose a suitable method and to provide participants with good feedback on what has been done with their input.

125774.explanation: D66 believes farmers should be able to earn extra income from other activities alongside their farm if they want and if possible. This keeps farming viable in the future.

125775.explanation: D66 wants the municipality to help residents with sustainable plans. Sometimes with money, but that is not necessary. Help can also come with knowledge or assistance in applying for permits. However, these sustainable plans must ultimately function well and be financially feasible.

125776.explanation: Space on the business parks in Vijfheerenlanden is limited. D66 wants to use that space smartly for jobs and a strong local economy. Large distribution centers do not contribute sufficiently to that.

125778.explanation: Employers who bring in foreign workers must arrange proper and adequate housing themselves. The municipality must also supervise this. Housing for seasonal workers may not come at the expense of regular housing for residents.

125782.explanation: D66 sees that one central fireworks display does not suit a sprawling municipality like Vijfheerenlanden. A show in every village is also not feasible. However, the municipality can help set up local fireworks clubs.

125783.explanation: D66 believes that the disposal of bulky waste should remain free of charge. For extra services, such as collecting bulky waste from home, a fee may be charged.

125786.explanation: If a farmer wants to invest in housing construction on their land and also takes the risks, that should be possible within the municipality's rules. The farmer is not allowed to do everything arbitrarily but must comply with the applicable regulations.

125787.explanation: D66 believes that everyone should be able to participate in society. Sports are healthy and promote connection. Children should not be excluded because their parents have less money.

125788.explanation: The local broadcaster must secure sufficient funding itself. That is not the municipality's task. The municipality can, however, purchase advertising space with the local broadcaster to share information with residents in this way.

125790.explanation: Music helps children learn better. Therefore, D66 considers it important that the municipality invests in music lessons. This way, we invest in the youth and the future. Cooperation with music associations from the municipality is the starting point.

125795.explanation: Camera surveillance infringes on your privacy. Therefore, it must always be carefully considered whether it is really necessary. D66 supports cameras only if they are used in places where there are risks and the safety demonstrably improves.

125802.explanation: D66 does not want to obligate people to do work. That can make it harder to find a suitable job. However, D66 supports helping and encouraging people on social assistance to do work that contributes something. That helps to gain rhythm, experience, and a good feeling.

125804.explanation: The municipality already has one of the highest income limits in the Netherlands. D66 thinks it's good to maintain that level, but further increases are pointless.

125806.explanation: There must be a good balance between charging stations for electric cars and regular parking spaces. In some locations, this means that additional charging points are needed.

125808.explanation: Because of staff shortages in healthcare, it is not always possible to get a care provider of choice. Residents may always express their preference. The care organization will then consider whether this can be accommodated.

125813.explanation: There are enough business parks in Vijfheerenlanden. D66 wants to use these areas better and smarter. Expansion should only take place if it is truly necessary.

125814.explanation: D66 supports shops being open on Sundays. Entrepreneurs in Vijfheerenlanden may choose themselves whether they want to be open then or not.

125817.explanation: There is currently too much solar energy and too little wind energy. D66 believes we must focus more on wind. That also generates electricity when the sun isn't shining. Additionally, it requires less space, allowing farmers to continue their work.

125821.explanation: A new coffee shop ensures that people can buy their cannabis safely, outside of criminal circuits. This reduces nuisance and makes it less likely for people to come into contact with hard drugs.

125823.explanation: D66 believes taller buildings are allowed if it fits the environment. For example, adding an extra floor to an existing apartment building or building higher after demolition. This is easier in larger towns like Leerdam and Vianen than in smaller villages such as Meerkerk.

125825.explanation: Larger villages in Vijfheerenlanden already have a village center. That is important for livability and must remain so.

125828.explanation: If P3 is expanded, Voorstraat can become car-free. This will make it a pleasant place to stay. Residents will then visit the center more quickly and stay longer. This brings more life to the street and more customers to shops and hospitality.

125830.explanation: By properly dispersing asylum seekers across the municipality, they can better participate in the neighborhood or village. Small-scale shelter works better and is supported more often than large shelter locations.

125770.explanation: Aldermen are, in principle, reliable and expert. The executive board preferably reflects the diversity of society.

125771.explanation: Residents should always have a say and be able to think along about important matters. Participation is a prerequisite for decision-making. A good method for this is a citizen’s assembly.

125774.explanation: We welcome the broadening of agricultural business activities to keep the agricultural sector vital and economically healthy.

125775.explanation: Locally generating and using sustainable energy helps reduce network congestion and contributes to our sustainability goals. Therefore, the municipality should encourage this as much as possible.

125776.explanation: We want to halt the so-called 'concretization' of our landscape to save space for nature, housing, and sustainable agriculture.

125778.explanation: Housing for seasonal workers or labor migrants must not come at the expense of the available housing stock for our residents.

125782.explanation: The municipality takes the initiative to organize a fireworks show together with residents in every village or town where there is a need.

125783.explanation: This is a basic requirement to prevent littering.

125786.explanation: We welcome building homes at agricultural businesses to keep the agricultural sector vital and economically healthy, and it helps combat the housing shortage.

125787.explanation: Sports not only make people fitter but also mentally healthier and stronger. This helps people continue participating in society and gives them better opportunities to develop themselves.

125788.explanation: Local and independent journalism strengthens local democracy and this should be encouraged.

125790.explanation: Music lessons are important because they stimulate brain development (memory, attention, self-regulation, language) and promote socio-emotional growth (self-confidence, cooperation, emotion regulation).

125795.explanation: Since the cameras at Leerdam station were removed, the number of stolen bicycles and vandalism has increased again.

125802.explanation: We trust people and see social work as an opportunity to get back to a permanent job.

125804.explanation: We want to increase basic security. Poverty is a political choice!

125806.explanation: The number of parking spaces for charging electric cars must keep pace with their use to prevent vacancies in parking spaces.

125808.explanation: This is part of an inclusive society.

125813.explanation: Sustainable business parks provide jobs and employment opportunities.

125814.explanation: Every entrepreneur must have the choice to open on Sundays.

125817.explanation: Solar panels on agricultural or farmland reduce the vitality of the rural area. Moreover, business cases for solar on land are virtually unfeasible. First, utilize empty roofs.

125821.explanation: We have one coffee shop, and that is sufficient for Vijfheerenlanden.

125823.explanation: The space available for building is becoming increasingly limited, and high-rise buildings are a solution for this.

125828.explanation: The Voorstraat in Vianen should be car-free at certain times because it is currently dangerous.

125830.explanation: We strive for distribution within the municipality, prioritizing the safety and well-being of residents and asylum seekers. Larger reception locations have more facilities such as security, daytime activities, and the like.

125770.explanation: We find representation of women and men, young and old, black, white, and everything in between important. It's about balance — a diverse group of people in the council, focusing especially on the capabilities and qualities someone has for the position.

125771.explanation: The voice of the residents is very important, but we cannot hold a referendum for every issue. Therefore, the first step should be a citizen panel, which continuously involves residents in politics and decision-making, making a referendum unnecessary.

125774.explanation: The municipality should not always allow this by default but may relax the rules for it. For example, traffic safety must be maintained. If you open a campsite arbitrarily, there will be more traffic movements, which could compromise traffic safety.

125775.explanation: The municipality should encourage residents to use sustainable energy. Currently, sustainable energy is unaffordable for residents with low incomes, so this would be a good outcome for them.

125776.explanation: The municipality can allow this, provided it is safe, fits within the zoning plan, and residents do not experience nuisance.

125778.explanation: This should not be an obligation but an option, and the municipality can encourage this by financially supporting entrepreneurs.

125782.explanation: This way we create connection on a lovely evening of the year. Perhaps with some nice stalls offering hot chocolate and an ice rink, a real celebration for residents. Additionally, this might compensate for the fireworks ban and encourage more residents to comply.

125783.explanation: This already happens but must continue. This way, people who are not mobile enough can still dispose of bulky waste, preventing illegal dumping.

125786.explanation: This should be made possible so we can encourage farmers to stop their work and possibly rent or sell these homes to residents of Vijfheerenlanden. However, the homes must meet certain exterior standards.

125787.explanation: As far as we're concerned, this should be tailored. When there is a need and someone cannot afford it for a very good reason, the municipality must step in.

125788.explanation: This is very important for the local community, but the municipality already funds the local broadcaster, and during a period of cutbacks, this does not have priority.

125790.explanation: This is good because subsidized music lessons provide more equal opportunities for all children, regardless of their parents' income. Music is important for children's cognitive, social, and emotional development. Additionally, it strengthens local culture and engagement.

125795.explanation: This is tailored and only necessary in locations where incidents have occurred or at high-risk locations for incidents.

125802.explanation: We believe this should be tailored. People on social assistance can participate in socially meaningful work, if it suits their capabilities, to stay involved and develop skills. Avres is an important organization in this.

125804.explanation: More and more middle incomes are falling into debt due to rising prices. This also affects families where both parents work. If we raise this limit, those families who urgently need help will receive it.

125806.explanation: In our opinion, there are now enough, and several are vacant during the day. This means there is sufficient supply. However, if residents in a particular neighborhood do miss a charging station, the municipality can of course designate some.

125808.explanation: Where possible, yes, but this is a matter over which the municipality has little influence. Where possible, the municipality must take this into account.

125813.explanation: There is a need for space for local entrepreneurs and employment. New business parks are possible if carefully integrated, fitting the landscape and giving priority to local businesses over large-scale developments.

125814.explanation: Shopping Sundays can contribute to liveliness but must not come at the expense of small businesses and employees. The municipality should allow local choices and tailor arrangements per center, in consultation with entrepreneurs. Politics should not interfere with this decision.

125817.explanation: Solar panels on agricultural land can contribute to sustainable energy production. The municipality should provide opportunities, provided that landscape integration and food production remain balanced.

125821.explanation: The municipality must pay attention to public order, safety, and livability. A new coffee shop is only negotiable with clear necessity, strict conditions, and support in the neighborhood.

125823.explanation: High-rise residential buildings do not fit everywhere in Vijfheerenlanden. Densification is sometimes necessary but must be done carefully and align with the village character, landscape, and livability of the area.

125825.explanation: Village centers are important for livability and social cohesion. Each larger village should have the chance to have its own village center so residents can gather and local activities can take place effectively.

125828.explanation: Making the Voorstraat car-free or low-traffic is the best option; making the entire center car-free is impossible because residents would not be able to reach their homes. Participation among residents, entrepreneurs, and the municipality is very important in this.

125830.explanation: Dispersed reception prevents overburdening one neighborhood and promotes integration. The municipality must choose locations carefully, with residents' support and attention to safety and livability. On the other hand, it may become more expensive—who will pay for that?

125770.explanation: It is important that women are well represented in the council, but fixed quotas are not leading. For BBV, suitability, quality, and trust are paramount, not enforcing numbers.

125771.explanation: BBV believes residents should have more direct say in major decisions. A local referendum can help build support and better explain decisions, as long as it is not a replacement for good governance and careful participation.

125774.explanation: Farmers must have space for side activities like a shop or campsite because this strengthens their business model. This requires tailored solutions, good landscape integration, and coordination with the surroundings.

125775.explanation: Local initiatives for sustainable energy deserve support, provided they are small scale and fit the landscape and environment. The municipality should not provide financial support for large wind turbines or solar fields that compromise agricultural land and livability.

125776.explanation: Large distribution centers often do not fit the landscape and scale of Vijfheerenlanden and put additional pressure on space, traffic, and livability. The municipality must be cautious and choose business activities that fit the environment and local economy.

125778.explanation: Employers are responsible for proper housing for seasonal workers, preferably on or near their own farms. The municipality should actively facilitate this, for example by cooperating and being flexible with permits.

125782.explanation: New Year's Eve is primarily a moment for and by the community. The municipality does not need to organize this centrally but can support local initiatives if there is a need.

125783.explanation: It is desirable that bulky waste can be collected easily, especially for people who cannot bring it themselves. This requires an affordable and fair system, not necessarily unlimited free collection for everyone.

125786.explanation: Farmers should be able to build homes on their own land under clear conditions, for example for family, caregiving, or business succession. This strengthens the rural area and livability.

125787.explanation: Sports and exercise are important for health and participation. The municipality can support people with low incomes, but this requires tailored and targeted arrangements, not a general obligation.

125788.explanation: A strong local broadcaster is important for information and involvement. Support can be appropriate but must always be weighed within a transparent and responsible financial framework.

125790.explanation: Music education is valuable for the development of children. The municipality can stimulate and support this where necessary, but this requires targeted choices and customization, not a general subsidy scheme for everyone.

125795.explanation: Camera surveillance can help with safety, but only targeted and in places where it is needed. No generic expansion, but tailored measures with attention to privacy and effectiveness.

125802.explanation: Those receiving social welfare benefits should be able to participate to the best of their ability by doing socially meaningful work. This helps people stay active and increases the chance of employment, provided personal circumstances are taken into account.

125804.explanation: The costs of housing and living have risen sharply. By raising the income threshold, the municipality can also support people who currently fall just outside the schemes but still need assistance.

125806.explanation: Not everyone can drive electrically. Continuously designating more parking spaces as charging spots comes at the expense of regular parking spaces and causes inequality and frustration. The municipality must handle this cautiously and balanced.

125808.explanation: It is important that care matches people's backgrounds and beliefs as much as possible. However, this cannot always be guaranteed due to a shortage of care providers. Freedom of choice where possible, realism where necessary.

125813.explanation: The municipality must manage space carefully. There should be room for local entrepreneurs where necessary, but expanding industrial areas is not an end in itself and must not come at the expense of the landscape and livability.

125814.explanation: Entrepreneurs must be able to decide for themselves whether to open on Sundays. The municipality should provide space where there is demand, but not impose a general obligation or expansion.

125817.explanation: Agricultural land is scarce and intended for food production. Solar panels belong on roofs and existing buildings, not at the expense of agricultural land and the landscape.

125821.explanation: A new coffeeshop does not fit the scale and livability of Vijfheerenlanden and could lead to extra nuisance and safety problems.

125823.explanation: High residential flats do not fit every town center. The municipality must weigh spatial and social suitability per location, respecting the character of villages and cities.

125825.explanation: Village community centers are important for livability and meeting places. Not only larger villages but also smaller communities deserve an appropriate facility. This requires customization, not a strict boundary.

125828.explanation: Livability in the center is important, but complete car-free zones go too far. The municipality should consider tailored solutions, allowing accessibility, entrepreneurs, and residents.

125830.explanation: The reception of asylum seekers must fit the scale, capacity, and safety of communities. Dispersion is not an objective in itself; customization and local consideration are necessary.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
There should be more camera surveillance in Vijfheerenlanden.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The residents of Vijfheerenlanden should be able to express their opinion on important issues through a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Vijfheerenlanden moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Large warehouses where goods are received, sorted and transported to other locations.

statements.title: The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
More business/industrial parks should be developed in Vijfheerenlanden.
Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The municipality should allow more Sunday shopping days.
The municipality should allow the establishment of a new coffeeshop (cannabis coffee shop).

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: All larger villages in the municipality should have their own village centre.
The center of Vianen should become car-free.
More high-rise residential apartment buildings should be allowed in Vijfheerenlanden.
The reception of asylum seekers should be spread across the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: People who have applied for asylum and are still waiting for a decision.

titleParts.text: moet verspreid worden over de gemeente.
